insights.title: How to Clear
Cancel Stealth, Get Q Back
Hidden broken mechanic
insights.reason:
Unironically, what every new Shaco player gets wrong is pressing their <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> because no one knows what the passive does.

* Your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> while off cooldown gives all your auto attacks a slow effect for 2 seconds. This is why <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Approach Velocity" /> is OP.
* This also applies to your Clone. So as long as your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> is up and your Clone is hitting someone, they are slowed, just tanking it, which is BROKEN combined with our earlier <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Hail of Blades" /> tip, since the clone is just going bananas on them.
* Only use <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> to finish kills. Your <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> also benefits from your passive backstab, so it's better to <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> as they try to get away at low HP.

approach-velocity.insight: Shaco has a permanent slow on his E, which means he can have 100% up-time from Approach Velocity.\n\n* Your clone can trigger it too. So even if you're not in range, if your clone is hitting them, it helps you catch up with the extra move-speed.

hail-of-blades.insight: Shaco deals extra damage when striking enemies from the back. Hail of Blades pairs perfectly with his passive, to stealth in and try to burst with 3 super quick autos before they react and turn around.\n\n* The omega OP tech for is to come out of stealth with a Q auto, activating Hail of Blades. Then press . Your clone will keep Hail of Blades' attack speed buff for the entire duration it's out. If players ignore the clone, it will solo them with this buff.

sorcery-alt.insight: Can swap to Gathering Storm and Absolute Focus as your secondary runes if you want to play for max scaling. \n\n* Much better in lower elo brackets where games go longer, and the early game is a bit less important.

itemGroups.insight: Sit on <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Magical Footwear" /> until you complete <ScItemInline itemName="Umbral Glaive" /> and <ScItemInline itemName="Voltaic Cyclosword" /> then upgrade.\n\nCan swap to <ScItemInline itemName="Boots of Swiftness" /> vs lots of slows, or <ScItemInline itemName="Mercury's Treads" /> <ScItemInline itemName="Plated Steelcaps" /> as needed.
itemGroups.title: Core Build
Fourth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Serylda's Grudge" /> vs some armor like <ScItemInline itemName="Plated Steelcaps" />\n\n<ScItemInline itemName="Lord Dominik's Regards" /> vs very tanky teams. <ScItemInline itemName="Serpent's Fang" /> vs shields.

itemGroups.title: Fifth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: You want % Armor Pen from <ScItemInline itemName="Serylda's Grudge" /> or <ScItemInline itemName="Lord Dominik's Regards" /> if you don't have it yet. <ScItemInline itemName="Edge of Night" /> for survivability

itemGroups.title: Sixth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Edge of Night" /> for defense, or <ScItemInline itemName="Infinity Edge" /> for max burst. Crit build details below.
